input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.reasoningText:
Now I need to:
1. Check if .NET 9 and .NET 10 are available
2. Create a BenchmarkDotNet project that targets both net9.0 and net10.0
3. Run the benchmarks

Let me check what .NET SDKs are installed.

data.content: Both .NET 9 and .NET 10 runtimes are available. Let me create a BenchmarkDotNet project that targets both frameworks.

toolCalls.name: create

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-63fc68dd0d8d44eda4491806600f5d60/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks.csproj b/tmp/sv-63fc68dd0d8d44eda4491806600f5d60/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks.csproj
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-63fc68dd0d8d44eda4491806600f5d60/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks.csproj
@@ -1,0 +1,15 @@
+<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
+  <PropertyGroup>
+    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
+    <TargetFrameworks>net9.0;net10.0</TargetFrameworks>
+    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
+    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
+  </PropertyGroup>
+  <ItemGroup>
+    <PackageReference Include="BenchmarkDotNet" Version="0.14.0" />
+  </ItemGroup>
+  <ItemGroup>
+    <Compile Include="..\TextAnalyzer\TextAnalyzer.cs" Link="TextAnalyzer.cs" />
+  </ItemGroup>
+</Project>
+

data.content: Let me fix the runtime moniker - BDN 0.14.0 may not have .NET 10 support. I'll use CLI args instead.
data.reasoningText:
BenchmarkDotNet 0.14.0 might not have Net100 moniker. Let me check what's available. I'll use a different approach - maybe use `Net10_0` or just use the attribute-based approach with custom job configs.

Actually, let me check the BDN source - in newer versions, .NET 10 might be `Net100` or might not exist yet in 0.14.0. Let me try a different approach using `.WithId()` and custom runtime configuration, or just use the `[SimpleJob]` with environment variable approach, or better yet use the `--runtimes` CLI argument.
 
The simplest solution is to skip the job attributes entirely and pass the runtimes through the command line instead.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-63fc68dd0d8d44eda4491806600f5d60/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks && dotnet run -c Release --framework net10.0 -- --filter '*' --runtimes net9.0 net10.0 2>&1

data.content: BDN 0.14.0 doesn't know about .NET 10. I'll run it separately on each framework using the host process runtime, then compare results.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-63fc68dd0d8d44eda4491806600f5d60/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks && echo "=== .NET 9 ===" && dotnet run -c Release --framework net9.0 -- --filter '*' 2>&1 | tail -20

data.reasoningText:
BDN 0.14.0 doesn't support .NET 10. I need to use a newer version or a different approach. Let me check if there's a newer BDN version that supports .NET 10, or I can use a manual config approach.

Actually, the best approach here since BDN 0.14.0 doesn't know about .NET 10 is to:
1. Run benchmarks twice - once with net9.0 framework and once with net10.0 framework, using the `hostprocess` runtime option (which benchmarks whatever framework the host is running on).

This way, BDN doesn't need to know about .NET 10 - it just runs on whatever runtime the host process uses.
